rawText:
I'm a storyteller. And I would like to tell you a few personal stories about what I like to call "the danger of the single story."

I grew up on a university campus in eastern Nigeria. My mother says that I started reading at the age of two, although I think four is probably close to the truth. So I was an early reader, and what I read were British and American children's books.

I was also an early writer, and when I began to write, at about the age of seven, stories in pencil with crayon illustrations that my poor mother was obligated to read, I wrote exactly the kinds of stories I was reading: All my characters were white and blue-eyed, they played in the snow, they ate apples, and they talked a lot about the weather, how lovely it was that the sun had come out.

Now, this despite the fact that I lived in Nigeria. I had never been outside Nigeria. We didn't have snow, we ate mangoes, and we never talked about the weather, because there was no need to.

What this demonstrates, I think, is how impressionable and vulnerable we are in the face of a story, particularly as children. Because all I had read were books in which characters were foreign, I had become convinced that books by their very nature had to have foreigners in them and had to be about things with which I could not personally identify.

Now, things changed when I discovered African books. There weren't many of them available, and they weren't quite as easy to find as the foreign books. But because of writers like Chinua Achebe and Camara Laye, I went through a mental shift in my perception of literature. I realized that people like me, girls with skin the color of chocolate, whose kinky hair could not form ponytails, could also exist in literature.

Now, I loved those American and British books I read. They stirred my imagination. They opened up new worlds for me. But the unintended consequence was that I did not know that people like me could exist in literature. So what the discovery of African writers did for me was this: It saved me from having a single story of what books are.

I come from a conventional, middle-class Nigerian family. My father was a professor. My mother was an administrator. And so we had, as was the norm, live-in domestic help, who would often come from nearby rural villages.

So, the year I turned eight, we got a new house boy. His name was Fide. The only thing my mother told us about him was that his family was very poor. My mother sent yams and rice, and our old clothes, to his family. And when I didn't finish my dinner, my mother would say, "Finish your food! Don't you know? People like Fide's family have nothing." So I felt enormous pity for Fide's family.

Then one Saturday, we went to his village to visit, and his mother showed us a beautifully patterned basket made of dyed raffia that his brother had made. I was startled. It had not occurred to me that anybody in his family could actually make something. All I had heard about them was how poor they were, so that it had become impossible for me to see them as anything else but poor. Their poverty was my single story of them.

overview.title: The Danger of a Single Story

sentences.original: I'm a storyteller.
sentences.translation: 저는 이야기꾼입니다.

vocabulary.example: She is a great storyteller who captivates her audience.

patterns.explanation: 자신의 정체성이나 직업을 간결하게 소개하는 패턴. 연설이나 프레젠테이션의 첫 문장으로 매우 효과적이다.
examples: I'm a teacher.
I'm a researcher in biology.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ a writer and a mother.

sentences.notes: 연설의 첫 문장. 짧고 강렬한 자기소개로 청중의 관심을 끄는 화법이다. TED 토크에서 자주 쓰이는 오프닝 기법.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 간결체(brevity) — 짧은 선언문으로 청중의 주의를 집중시킴

sentences.original: And I would like to tell you a few personal stories about what I like to call "the danger of the single story."
sentences.translation: 그리고 저는 제가 "단일한 이야기의 위험"이라고 부르는 것에 대한 몇 가지 개인적인 이야기를 들려드리고 싶습니다.

grammarElements.text: about what I like to call "the danger of the single story"
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: She shared a personal experience with the audience.
There is a danger of oversimplifying the issue.

expressions.usage: want to보다 정중한 표현으로 공식적 자리에서 사용
자신만의 용어나 개념을 소개할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.explanation: 자신이 명명한 개념이나 용어를 소개하는 패턴. 청중에게 새로운 프레임을 제시할 때 사용한다.
examples: This is what I like to call "the feedback loop."
It's what I like to call "a learning moment."

drillQuestions.question: This is _____ I like to call "the ripple effect."

sentences.notes: "what I like to call"은 화자가 주제에 이름을 붙이는 표현으로, 연설 전체의 키워드("the danger of the single story")를 도입한다. about 이하의 관계절 구조에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: And I would like to tell you
about what I like to call

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 주제 선언(thesis statement) — 연설의 핵심 주제를 명확히 제시

sentences.original: I grew up on a university campus in eastern Nigeria.
sentences.translation: 저는 나이지리아 동부의 대학교 캠퍼스에서 자랐습니다.

vocabulary.example: She grew up in a small town.
The library is located on the main campus.

expressions.usage: 자신이 어린 시절을 보낸 장소를 말할 때

patterns.explanation: 자신의 성장 배경을 소개하는 기본 패턴. 개인적 이야기의 도입부에 자주 사용된다.
examples: I grew up in a small village.
I grew up on a farm in the countryside.

drillQuestions.question: She grew _____ in a big city.

sentences.notes: 개인 배경 소개의 시작. "on a campus"에서 on은 특정 부지/구역 위에 있다는 의미로 사용된다.

sentences.original: My mother says that I started reading at the age of two, although I think four is probably close to the truth.
sentences.translation: 어머니는 제가 두 살에 읽기를 시작했다고 말씀하시지만, 저는 네 살이 아마 진실에 가까울 거라고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: that I started reading at the age of two
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (that절)

grammarElements.text: although I think four is probably close to the truth
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사절 (although)

vocabulary.example: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
He will probably arrive late.

expressions.usage: 특정 나이를 명시할 때 사용하는 격식적 표현
완전히 정확하지는 않지만 거의 맞다는 것을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 누군가의 말을 인용한 후 although로 대조되는 자신의 견해를 덧붙이는 패턴. 접속사 although의 핵심 용법.
examples: He says that the project is finished, although I think there is still more work to do.
The report says that sales increased, although the data suggests otherwise.

drillQuestions.question: She claims it was easy, _____ I think it was quite difficult.

sentences.notes: although는 Week 3의 핵심 학습 포인트인 종속접속사. 주절과 양보절을 연결한다. 유머러스한 자기 비하로 청중과의 거리를 좁히는 화법.

koreanAlignment.en: four is probably close to the truth
koreanAlignment.ko: 네 살이 아마 진실에 가까울 거라고

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 자기 비하 유머(self-deprecation) — 과장된 주장을 유머로 수정하며 친근감 형성

sentences.original: So I was an early reader, and what I read were British and American children's books.
sentences.translation: 그래서 저는 일찍 책을 읽기 시작했고, 제가 읽은 것은 영국과 미국의 아동 도서였습니다.

vocabulary.example: She was an early reader who loved fairy tales.

expressions.usage: 관계대명사 what을 주어로 사용하여 강조하는 구문

patterns.explanation: 관계대명사 what이 이끄는 명사절을 주어로 사용하는 강조 구문. "내가 ~한 것은 바로 ...이었다."
examples: What I needed was a good night's sleep.
What she said was absolutely true.

drillQuestions.question: _____ I wanted was a simple answer.

sentences.notes: "what I read"는 관계대명사 what이 이끄는 명사절로 주어 역할을 한다. Week 3 핵심인 관계대명사 학습에 중요한 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: So I was an early reader
koreanAlignment.ko: 그래서 저는 일찍 책을 읽기 시작했고

koreanAlignment.en: were British and American children's books

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조 설정(setup for contrast) — 뒤에 나올 나이지리아 현실과의 대비를 준비

sentences.original: I was also an early writer, and when I began to write, at about the age of seven, stories in pencil with crayon illustrations that my poor mother was obligated to read, I wrote exactly the kinds of stories I was reading: All my characters were white and blue-eyed, they played in the snow, they ate apples, and they talked a lot about the weather, how lovely it was that the sun had come out.
sentences.translation: 저는 또한 일찍 글쓰기를 시작했고, 약 일곱 살에 연필로 쓰고 크레용으로 삽화를 그린 이야기들을 — 불쌍한 어머니가 읽어야만 했던 — 쓰기 시작했을 때, 저는 정확히 제가 읽고 있던 종류의 이야기를 썼습니다: 모든 등장인물은 백인에 파란 눈이었고, 눈 속에서 놀았고, 사과를 먹었고, 날씨에 대해 많이 이야기했습니다, 해가 나와서 얼마나 좋은지.

grammarElements.text: that my poor mother was obligated to read
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that)

grammarElements.text: I wrote exactly the kinds of stories I was reading
grammarElements.label: 주절

vocabulary.example: The book was filled with beautiful illustrations.
Parents feel obligated to help their children.
The main character in the novel is a young girl.

expressions.usage: 유형이나 종류를 나타낼 때 사용하는 표현
감탄을 나타내는 간접 표현

patterns.pattern: when I began to [verb], ... I [verb]ed exactly ...
patterns.explanation: when 종속절로 시간을 설정한 후 주절에서 핵심 행동을 기술하는 패턴. 복합문의 전형적 구조.
examples: When I started to learn English, I watched exactly the same shows my classmates were watching.

drillQuestions.question: _____ I began to cook, I made exactly the dishes my grandmother used to make.

sentences.notes: 매우 긴 복합문. when절, that 관계절, 콜론(:) 이후의 나열 등 여러 종속절이 결합되어 있다. 삽입구(at about the age of seven, stories in pencil...)가 주절을 지연시켜 긴장감을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: I was also an early writer
at about the age of seven
that my poor mother was obligated to read
I wrote exactly the kinds of stories I was reading
koreanAlignment.ko: 저는 정확히 제가 읽고 있던 종류의 이야기를 썼습니다

koreanAlignment.en: All my characters were white and blue-eyed
they talked a lot about the weather

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 나열법(enumeration) — "white and blue-eyed, played in the snow, ate apples"로 외국 문화 요소를 나열하여 아이러니를 강화

sentences.original: Now, this despite the fact that I lived in Nigeria.
sentences.translation: 그런데 이것은 제가 나이지리아에 살고 있었는데도 불구하고 그랬습니다.

grammarElements.text: despite the fact that I lived in Nigeria
grammarElements.label: 양보 전치사구 + 동격절

vocabulary.example: Despite the rain, they continued the game.

expressions.usage: despite 뒤에 절을 이어야 할 때 사용하는 격식 표현. although와 같은 의미.

patterns.explanation: "이것은 ~라는 사실에도 불구하고 그렇다"는 의미. 앞의 상황이 뒤의 사실과 모순됨을 강조한다.
examples: She won the race, this despite the fact that she had been injured.
The company grew, this despite the fact that the economy was weak.

drillQuestions.question: He passed the exam, _____ the fact that he barely studied.

sentences.notes: "despite the fact that"은 although와 같은 의미이지만 더 강조적이다. "Now"는 여기서 시간이 아니라 담화 전환을 위한 표지(discourse marker)로 사용되었다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역접(contrast) — 영국풍 이야기를 쓴 것과 나이지리아에 살았다는 사실의 아이러니를 강조

sentences.original: I had never been outside Nigeria.
sentences.translation: 저는 나이지리아 밖으로 나가 본 적이 없었습니다.

vocabulary.example: He waited outside the building.

patterns.explanation: 과거완료(had + p.p.)와 never를 결합하여 "그때까지 한 번도 ~한 적이 없었다"는 경험의 부재를 표현하는 패턴.
examples: I had never seen snow before that winter.
She had never traveled abroad before college.

drillQuestions.question: Before moving to Seoul, I _____ never been to Korea.

sentences.notes: 과거완료(had never been)를 사용하여 글을 쓰던 시점보다 이전의 경험 부재를 나타낸다. 과거완료는 "과거보다 더 과거"를 표현하는 시제.

sentences.original: We didn't have snow, we ate mangoes, and we never talked about the weather, because there was no need to.
sentences.translation: 우리에게는 눈이 없었고, 우리는 망고를 먹었고, 날씨에 대해 이야기한 적이 없었습니다, 왜냐하면 그럴 필요가 없었기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: and we never talked about the weather
grammarElements.label: 등위절

grammarElements.text: because there was no need to
grammarElements.label: 이유 부사절 (because)

vocabulary.example: Fresh mangoes are available in the summer.

expressions.usage: 어떤 행동이 불필요했음을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 세 개의 등위절을 나열한 후 because로 이유를 덧붙이는 복합문 구조. 나열과 인과를 결합.
examples: We didn't go shopping, we stayed home, and we watched movies, because it was raining.

drillQuestions.question: We stayed inside _____ there was a storm outside.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 영국풍 요소(snow, apples, weather)와 대비되는 나이지리아 현실(no snow, mangoes, no weather talk)을 나열하여 아이러니를 극대화한다. because 종속절의 기본 용법.

koreanAlignment.en: and we never talked about the weather
because there was no need to

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대비법(antithesis) — 영국 문화와 나이지리아 현실의 극적 대비

sentences.original: What this demonstrates, I think, is how impressionable and vulnerable we are in the face of a story, particularly as children.
sentences.translation: 이것이 보여주는 것은, 제 생각에, 우리가 이야기 앞에서 얼마나 감수성이 풍부하고 취약한지, 특히 어린이로서 그렇다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: how impressionable and vulnerable we are
grammarElements.label: 보어 (how절)

grammarElements.text: in the face of a story
grammarElements.label: 전치사구

vocabulary.example: The experiment demonstrates the theory.
Young children are very impressionable.
Children are particularly vulnerable to manipulation.

expressions.usage: 어떤 상황이나 힘에 직면했을 때를 표현하는 관용구
특정 조건이나 신분을 강조할 때

patterns.explanation: 관계대명사 what절 + be동사 + how절의 결합. "이것이 보여주는 것은 얼마나 ~한지이다"라는 의미.
examples: What this shows is how important education is.
What the study reveals is how fragile the ecosystem is.

drillQuestions.question: _____ this experiment shows is how dangerous the chemical can be.

sentences.notes: Week 3 핵심인 관계대명사 what의 고급 용법. what절이 주어, how절이 보어인 복합 구조. 삽입절 "I think"이 중간에 들어가 자연스러운 구어체 효과를 낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: is how impressionable and vulnerable we are
in the face of a story

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 성찰적 일반화(reflective generalization) — 개인 경험에서 보편적 진리를 도출

sentences.original: Because all I had read were books in which characters were foreign, I had become convinced that books by their very nature had to have foreigners in them and had to be about things with which I could not personally identify.
sentences.translation: 왜냐하면 제가 읽은 것이 모두 등장인물이 외국인인 책들이었기 때문에, 저는 책이란 본질적으로 외국인이 나와야 하고 제가 개인적으로 공감할 수 없는 것들에 관한 것이어야 한다고 확신하게 되었습니다.

grammarElements.text: Because all I had read were books in which characters were foreign
grammarElements.label: 이유 부사절 (because) + 관계대명사 in which

grammarElements.text: that books by their very nature had to have foreigners in them
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (that절)

grammarElements.text: and had to be about things with which I could not personally identify
grammarElements.label: 등위절 + 관계대명사 with which

vocabulary.example: The evidence convinced everyone.
These problems are, by their very nature, difficult to solve.
Readers often identify with the main character.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것의 근본적인 특성을 강조할 때
자신을 누군가와 같다고 느끼거나 연결감을 느낄 때

patterns.pattern: Because [reason clause], I had become convinced that [belief]
patterns.explanation: because 이유절로 원인을 제시하고, "확신하게 되었다"는 결과를 과거완료로 표현하는 패턴.
examples: Because I had only heard bad news, I had become convinced that the situation was hopeless.

drillQuestions.question: _____ she had only met unfriendly people, she had become convinced that the city was unwelcoming.

sentences.notes: Week 3 핵심 학습의 집약체. because 종속절, in which 관계대명사, that 명사절, with which 관계대명사가 모두 포함된 다층 복합문. "in which"와 "with which"는 전치사 + 관계대명사의 격식적 용법.

koreanAlignment.en: were books in which characters were foreign
had to have foreigners in them
with which I could not personally identify

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 인과적 서술(causal narrative) — 읽기 경험이 세계관 형성에 미친 인과관계를 정밀하게 추적

sentences.original: Now, things changed when I discovered African books.
sentences.translation: 그런데 제가 아프리카 책을 발견했을 때 상황이 바뀌었습니다.

vocabulary.example: She discovered a new author at the library.

expressions.usage: 이야기의 전환점을 나타낼 때

patterns.explanation: 이야기의 전환점(turning point)을 제시하는 패턴. when절로 변화의 계기를 설명한다.
examples: Things changed when she met her mentor.
Things changed when the company adopted new technology.

drillQuestions.question: Things changed _____ I started learning a second language.

sentences.notes: 짧지만 서사의 전환점(turning point)을 알리는 핵심 문장. "Now"는 시간이 아닌 담화 전환 표지로 반복 사용됨.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(volta) — 이야기의 방향을 바꾸는 전환점 표시

sentences.original: There weren't many of them available, and they weren't quite as easy to find as the foreign books.
sentences.translation: 많이 구할 수 있는 것은 아니었고, 외국 책만큼 쉽게 찾을 수 있지도 않았습니다.

grammarElements.text: they weren't quite as easy to find as the foreign books
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (as...as 비교)

vocabulary.example: This book is available at most bookstores.

expressions.usage: 두 대상을 비교하며 차이를 부드럽게 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: "~만큼 ~하지는 않다"는 완곡한 비교 표현. quite가 부정을 부드럽게 만든다.
examples: The sequel wasn't quite as good as the original.
The task wasn't quite as easy as I expected.

drillQuestions.question: The movie wasn't quite _____ interesting as the book.

sentences.notes: "not quite as...as" 비교 구문은 부정을 완곡하게 표현하는 방법. 직접적으로 "harder"라고 하지 않고 우회적으로 말하는 영어의 특징.

koreanAlignment.en: There weren't many of them available
koreanAlignment.ko: 많이 구할 수 있는 것은 아니었고

koreanAlignment.en: and they weren't quite as easy to find

sentences.original: But because of writers like Chinua Achebe and Camara Laye, I went through a mental shift in my perception of literature.
sentences.translation: 하지만 치누아 아체베와 카마라 라예 같은 작가들 덕분에, 저는 문학에 대한 인식에서 정신적 전환을 겪었습니다.

grammarElements.text: because of writers like Chinua Achebe and Camara Laye
grammarElements.label: 이유 전치사구 (because of + 명사)

vocabulary.example: Going abroad caused a mental shift in how I saw the world.
His perception of the problem changed over time.
She studied English literature at university.

expressions.usage: 변화나 어려운 과정을 경험할 때
because와 달리 뒤에 명사(구)가 온다

patterns.explanation: "~덕분에/때문에 ~를 겪었다"는 인과관계 표현. because of + 명사 vs because + 절의 차이를 이해하는 것이 중요.
examples: Because of my teacher, I went through a major transformation.
Because of the crisis, we went through significant changes.

drillQuestions.question: _____ of her advice, I changed my career.

sentences.notes: "because of"는 뒤에 명사(구)가 오고, "because"는 뒤에 절이 온다. 이 차이는 Week 3의 접속사 학습에서 매우 중요한 포인트.

koreanAlignment.en: like Chinua Achebe and Camara Laye

sentences.original: I realized that people like me, girls with skin the color of chocolate, whose kinky hair could not form ponytails, could also exist in literature.
sentences.translation: 저는 저 같은 사람들, 초콜릿 색 피부를 가진 소녀들, 곱슬곱슬한 머리카락으로 포니테일을 할 수 없는 소녀들도 문학 속에 존재할 수 있다는 것을 깨달았습니다.

grammarElements.text: girls with skin the color of chocolate
grammarElements.label: 동격 (people like me를 설명)

grammarElements.text: whose kinky hair could not form ponytails
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (whose)

vocabulary.example: I realized I had made a mistake.
She embraced her natural kinky hair.
Many species exist only in the rainforest.

expressions.usage: 자기와 유사한 부류의 사람들을 지칭할 때

patterns.explanation: that절 안에 whose 관계대명사절이 삽입된 다층 구조. "~가 ~할 수 있다는 것을 깨달았다."
examples: I realized that students, whose backgrounds were diverse, could all succeed.

drillQuestions.question: She realized that the artist, _____ paintings were beautiful, lived nearby.

sentences.notes: whose는 소유격 관계대명사로, "~의 머리카락이"라는 의미. 동격 구문(people like me = girls with skin...)과 whose 관계절이 결합된 고급 구조. Week 3 관계대명사 핵심 예문.

koreanAlignment.en: girls with skin the color of chocolate
whose kinky hair could not form ponytails
koreanAlignment.ko: 곱슬곱슬한 머리카락으로 포니테일을 할 수 없는
문학 속에 존재할 수 있다는 것을

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 구체적 묘사(vivid description) — "skin the color of chocolate", "kinky hair"로 추상적 개념을 감각적으로 전달

sentences.original: Now, I loved those American and British books I read.
sentences.translation: 저는 제가 읽은 미국과 영국 책들을 사랑했습니다.

grammarElements.text: those American and British books I read
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계대명사 생략 (that I read)

patterns.explanation: 목적격 관계대명사 that/which가 생략된 구조. "those books (that) I read"에서 that이 생략.
examples: The movie I watched was great.
The person you met is my friend.

drillQuestions.question: The book _____ I read was fascinating. (빈칸에 들어갈 수 있는 것은?)

sentences.notes: 목적격 관계대명사(that) 생략. "those books (that) I read"에서 that이 빠진 형태는 구어체에서 매우 일반적.

koreanAlignment.en: those American and British books I read

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 양보적 도입(concessive opening) — 비판이 아닌 감사에서 출발하여 균형 잡힌 관점을 보여줌

sentences.original: They stirred my imagination.
sentences.translation: 그 책들은 제 상상력을 자극했습니다.

vocabulary.example: The movie stirred deep emotions in the audience.
Children have a vivid imagination.

expressions.usage: 무언가가 창의적 영감이나 호기심을 불러일으킬 때

patterns.explanation: "~의 감정/능력을 자극하다"는 비유적 표현 패턴. stir는 원래 "젓다"인데 비유적으로 "자극하다"로 사용.
examples: The speech stirred their patriotism.
The painting stirred her curiosity.

drillQuestions.question: The music _____ his emotions deeply.

sentences.notes: stir는 "젓다"가 기본 의미이지만, 여기서는 "감정이나 상상력을 일깨우다"라는 은유적 의미. 짧은 문장으로 리듬감을 살린다.

sentences.original: But the unintended consequence was that I did not know that people like me could exist in literature.
sentences.translation: 하지만 의도하지 않은 결과는 저 같은 사람들이 문학 속에 존재할 수 있다는 것을 제가 몰랐다는 것이었습니다.

grammarElements.text: that people like me could exist in literature
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (이중 that절)

vocabulary.example: The policy had unintended consequences.
Every action has consequences.

expressions.usage: 어떤 행동이 예상치 못한 결과를 가져왔을 때

patterns.explanation: "~의 결과는 ~이었다"는 인과 관계 서술 패턴. that절이 보어로 사용됨.
examples: The unexpected consequence was that sales actually increased.
The natural consequence was that students lost interest.

drillQuestions.question: The unintended consequence _____ that many people lost their jobs.

sentences.notes: that절이 이중으로 사용됨: 첫 번째 that은 보어절, 두 번째 that은 know의 목적어절. 같은 접속사가 다른 기능으로 쓰이는 것에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: was that I did not know

koreanAlignment.ko: 문학 속에 존재할 수 있다는 것을

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반전(reversal) — 긍정적 경험(사랑한 책)의 이면에 숨은 부정적 결과를 밝힘

sentences.original: So what the discovery of African writers did for me was this: It saved me from having a single story of what books are.
sentences.translation: 그래서 아프리카 작가들의 발견이 저에게 해준 것은 이것이었습니다: 책이란 무엇인지에 대한 단일한 이야기를 갖는 것에서 저를 구해주었습니다.

grammarElements.text: what the discovery of African writers did for me
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사 what절)

grammarElements.text: It saved me from having a single story of what books are
grammarElements.label: 설명절

vocabulary.example: The discovery of new species excited the researchers.
The umbrella saved me from getting wet.

expressions.phrase: what ... did for me was this:
expressions.usage: 어떤 경험의 의미나 영향을 강조적으로 설명할 때
편견이나 고정관념을 비유적으로 표현할 때 (이 연설의 핵심 개념)

patterns.pattern: What [X] did for me was this: [explanation]
patterns.explanation: what절을 주어로 사용한 강조 구문에 콜론(:)으로 부연 설명을 추가하는 패턴.
examples: What the training did for me was this: It taught me discipline.
What travel did for her was this: It broadened her perspective.

drillQuestions.question: _____ the experience taught me was this: patience is a virtue.

sentences.notes: 관계대명사 what절이 주어로 사용된 강조 구문의 완성형. 콜론(:)으로 드라마틱한 정리를 제시. "a single story"라는 연설의 핵심 개념이 처음으로 구체적으로 적용됨.

koreanAlignment.en: So what the discovery of African writers did for me
koreanAlignment.ko: 그래서 아프리카 작가들의 발견이 저에게 해준 것은

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 요약과 주제 연결(summary and theme connection) — 개인 일화에서 핵심 주제 "single story"로의 연결

sentences.original: I come from a conventional, middle-class Nigerian family.
sentences.translation: 저는 전형적인 나이지리아 중산층 가정 출신입니다.

vocabulary.example: They live a conventional lifestyle.
She grew up in a middle-class neighborhood.

expressions.usage: 자신의 배경이나 출신을 소개할 때

patterns.explanation: 자신의 가족 배경을 여러 형용사로 수식하여 소개하는 패턴.
examples: I come from a large, traditional Korean family.
She comes from a wealthy, well-educated family.

drillQuestions.question: He comes _____ a small farming family.

sentences.notes: 새로운 일화를 시작하기 위한 배경 설정. "conventional"은 "평범한, 보통의"라는 뉘앙스로, 특별하지 않은 평범한 가정임을 강조.

sentences.original: So, the year I turned eight, we got a new house boy.
sentences.translation: 그래서 제가 여덟 살이 되던 해에, 우리는 새로운 가사 도우미를 얻었습니다.

vocabulary.example: She turned 30 last month.
The house boy helped with the daily chores.

expressions.usage: 특정 나이가 되었던 시점을 이야기할 때

patterns.explanation: "~가 ~살이 되던 해"를 나타내는 시간 표현. 관계부사 when이 생략된 형태.
examples: The year she turned sixteen, everything changed.
The year I turned forty, I started a new career.

drillQuestions.question: The year I _____ twelve, my family moved to a new city.

sentences.notes: "the year (when) I turned eight"에서 관계부사 when이 생략. 새 일화(Fide 이야기)의 시작.

sentences.original: And when I didn't finish my dinner, my mother would say, "Finish your food! Don't you know? People like Fide's family have nothing."
sentences.translation: 그리고 제가 저녁을 다 먹지 않으면, 어머니는 이렇게 말씀하시곤 했습니다. "음식을 다 먹어라! 모르겠니? 피데네 가족 같은 사람들은 아무것도 없단다."

grammarElements.text: when I didn't finish my dinner
grammarElements.label: 시간/조건 부사절 (when)

vocabulary.example: Please finish your homework before dinner.

expressions.usage: 과거의 반복적 행동이나 습관을 나타내는 would의 용법
상대방이 마땅히 알아야 할 것을 환기시킬 때 사용하는 수사적 질문

patterns.explanation: "~할 때마다, ~하곤 했다"는 과거 반복 습관을 나타내는 패턴. would는 "~하곤 했다"의 의미.
examples: When it rained, she would stay inside and read.
When I was young, my father would tell me stories.

drillQuestions.question: When I was late, my teacher _____ always scold me.

sentences.notes: would는 여기서 과거의 반복적 습관을 나타낸다 (used to와 유사). 직접 인용문을 통해 어머니의 말투를 생생하게 전달하는 스토리텔링 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: And when I didn't finish my dinner
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 제가 저녁을 다 먹지 않으면

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 직접 인용(direct quotation) — 어머니의 실제 말투를 재현하여 장면을 생생하게 만듦

sentences.original: Then one Saturday, we went to his village to visit, and his mother showed us a beautifully patterned basket made of dyed raffia that his brother had made.
sentences.translation: 그러다 어느 토요일, 우리는 그의 마을을 방문하러 갔고, 그의 어머니는 그의 형이 만든 염색된 라피아로 만든 아름다운 무늬의 바구니를 우리에게 보여주었습니다.

grammarElements.text: we went to his village to visit
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: a beautifully patterned basket made of dyed raffia
grammarElements.label: 직접목적어

vocabulary.example: She wore a brightly patterned dress.
The fabric was dyed in bright colors.
Raffia is commonly used for making baskets in West Africa.

expressions.usage: 재료를 나타낼 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 과거분사(made of)와 관계대명사 that절이 결합하여 명사를 이중으로 수식하는 구조.
examples: A scarf made of silk that her grandmother had knitted.
A table made of oak that his father had built.

drillQuestions.question: She showed me a necklace made _____ silver that her aunt had crafted.

sentences.notes: 이중 수식 구조: "basket"이 과거분사구(made of dyed raffia)와 관계대명사절(that his brother had made)로 동시에 수식됨. 이 바구니의 아름다움이 화자의 편견을 깨뜨리는 계기.

koreanAlignment.en: we went to his village to visit

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 구체적 디테일(specific detail) — 바구니의 재질과 무늬를 세밀히 묘사하여 발견의 순간을 강화

sentences.original: All I had heard about them was how poor they were, so that it had become impossible for me to see them as anything else but poor.
sentences.translation: 그들에 대해 제가 들은 것은 오직 그들이 얼마나 가난한지뿐이어서, 그들을 가난한 사람 외에 다른 무엇으로도 보는 것이 불가능해져 버렸습니다.

grammarElements.text: All I had heard about them
grammarElements.label: 주어 (All + 관계대명사 생략)

grammarElements.text: so that it had become impossible for me to see them as anything else but poor
grammarElements.label: 결과 부사절 (so that)

vocabulary.example: It seemed impossible to finish on time.

expressions.usage: 앞의 상황의 결과를 설명할 때
유일한 가능성을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: All I had [past participle] was [how clause], so that ...
patterns.explanation: "내가 ~한 전부는 ...이어서, 결과적으로 ~하게 되었다." All + 관계대명사 생략 + so that 결과절의 복합 구조.
examples: All I had seen was failure, so that I had lost all confidence.
All she had learned was theory, so that she struggled with practice.

drillQuestions.question: _____ I had known about the city was its crime rate, so I was afraid to visit.

sentences.notes: "All (that) I had heard"에서 관계대명사 that이 생략됨. so that 결과절이 편견의 형성 과정을 논리적으로 설명한다. "single story"의 작동 방식을 정확히 보여주는 핵심 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: All I had heard about them
koreanAlignment.ko: 그들에 대해 제가 들은 것은 전부

koreanAlignment.en: so that it had become impossible

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 인과적 논증(causal reasoning) — 편견이 형성되는 메커니즘을 단계적으로 보여줌

sentences.original: Their poverty was my single story of them.
sentences.translation: 그들의 가난이 그들에 대한 나의 단일한 이야기였습니다.

vocabulary.example: Many families live in poverty.

expressions.usage: 이 연설의 핵심 개념. 한 가지 관점으로만 대상을 보는 것을 비유

patterns.explanation: "~이 ~에 대한 나의 단일한 이야기였다." 연설의 핵심 메타포를 직접 적용하는 패턴.
examples: His fame was my single story of him.
Their wealth was the world's single story of them.

drillQuestions.question: Their success was my single _____ of them.

sentences.notes: 연설 전체 주제의 핵심 문장. 짧고 힘 있는 마무리로 이야기의 교훈을 압축. "single story"라는 메타포가 개인적 경험과 연결되는 순간.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 에피그램(epigram) — 짧고 기억에 남는 문장으로 핵심 메시지를 전달
